purpose: Per-bank parity audit verdict comparing 16 AST sanitizer banks vs their corresponding regex sanitizer Vecs. Closes the 2 gaps surfaced in M1/M2 so M4 can atomically delete the regex banks without coverage regression.
audit_method: Direct read+diff of each (regex Vec, AST bank) pair in crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs. Regex Vecs at L497..L767. AST banks at L1738..L2903. Verified call_node_kinds + extract_call_name_<lang> behavior to confirm structural shape availability.

TypeScript.fix: Added AstSanitizerPattern { call_names: [], member_patterns: [("*", "parse"), ("*", "safeParse")], sanitizer_type: Numeric }. Wildcard receiver matches via call-shape path in member_patterns_match (taint.rs:3077-3079). Same coverage surface as the regex it replaces (also catches JSON.parse/Date.parse — parity-preserving).

JavaScript.verdict: parity (shares TYPESCRIPT_PATTERNS / TYPESCRIPT_AST_SANITIZERS via get_patterns and get_ast_patterns dispatch)
JavaScript.notes: TypeScript fix above transparently covers JS.

Go.notes: Exact 1:1 parity. extract_call_name_go returns the dotted form so the call-shape path in member_patterns_match handles direct method-call shapes too.

Java.notes: 1:1 parity. Java method_invocation grammar with extract_call_name_java covers call-shape path.

ast_entries: member_patterns: ("", ".parse::<i32>") .. ("", ".parse::<isize>") -> Numeric (raw substring fallback for turbofish call shape)
Rust.notes: Turbofish .parse::<T> is generic_function shape; raw substring fallback intentional. String-literal regression risk identical to all other raw-fallback entries — gated by per-descendant is_in_string filter at caller.

C.notes: Pure call_names parity — type-annotation flip only.

Cpp.gap: M2-FIND-01: raw-substring fallback in member_patterns_match (taint.rs:3094-3098) walks descendant_text. For an assignment node containing a string-literal child (e.g. `std::string msg = "use std::stoi to convert";`), the assignment itself is NOT in_string, so the per-descendant is_in_string filter at L3508/L3564 does not exclude it. The substring match then fired inside the string literal contents.
Cpp.investigation_summary: Option A (structural ("std", "stoi")) cannot match because (1) extract_member_access_receiver_and_field on a qualified_identifier `std::stoi` does not yield a (rcv,field) pair the same way `obj.method` does, and (2) the call-shape path uses rfind('.'), which does not split on `::`. Modifying member_patterns_match to handle `::` is forbidden by standing rules. Option B (filter raw-substring fallback to skip nodes containing string-literal children) was rejected as too broad — would affect Ruby/Elixir/Rust/Java raw fallbacks and risk silent coverage loss. Option C chosen: move entries to call_names, which only fires on call_node_kinds descendants (call_expression for C++); string literals are not call_expressions, so the regression cannot recur.
Cpp.fix: Replaced two AstSanitizerPattern blocks: (1) std::sto* moved from member_patterns raw fallback to call_names: ["std::stoi","std::stol","std::stoul","std::stoll","std::stof","std::stod"]; (2) static_cast<T> moved to call_names: ["static_cast<int>","static_cast<long>","static_cast<float>","static_cast<double>"]. Both rely on extract_call_name_c returning the function-field text verbatim (qualified_identifier yields `std::stoi`; template_function yields `static_cast<int>`). Verified by `cpp_std_stoi_sanitizer_truncates_flow_via_compute_taint` PASS (sanitizer still fires on real call) and `cpp_std_stoi_in_string_literal_does_not_sanitize` PASS (string-literal regression closed).

ast_entries: member_patterns: ("", "CGI.escapeHTML") ("", "Rack::Utils.escape_html") -> Html (raw fallback for module-method calls)
Ruby.notes: 1:1 parity. Wildcard handles any obj.to_i. Raw fallback for module-prefixed methods accepted carry-forward (well-tested, no string-literal collision in M1 fixtures).

CSharp.notes: 1:1 parity. extract_call_name_csharp on invocation_expression returns dotted form for call-shape path.

ast_entries: call_names: intval/floatval + member_patterns: ("","(int)") ("","(float)") -> Numeric (cast expression raw fallback intentional)
Php.notes: 1:1 parity. (int)/(float) casts intentionally retained as raw fallback per the existing pattern; cast_expression nodes are the descendant-kind those substrings appear under.

Lua.notes: Pure call_names parity. Lua/Luau share LUA_PATTERNS via get_patterns dispatch.

ast_entries: member_patterns: ("","String.to_integer") ("","String.to_float") -> Numeric (raw fallback for Module.function form)
Elixir.notes: 1:1 parity via raw fallback (intentional per Elixir module-method shape; field_access_info-extension-v1 M5 documented carry-forward).

additions_made.rationale: Gap 2 (M1 finding). The TYPESCRIPT_PATTERNS regex bank includes `\.(parse|safeParse)\s*\(` -> Numeric for Zod-style schema validators (`schema.parse(input)` / `schema.safeParse(input)`). The AST bank lacked an equivalent; once M4 deletes the regex bank, any TS/JS code using Zod sanitization would lose detection. Wildcard receiver ("*") matches via the call-shape path in member_patterns_match (taint.rs:3077-3079), preserving exact regex coverage surface (including the over-broad JSON.parse/Date.parse matches the regex also catches — parity-preserving).

entries_modified: Replaced two AstSanitizerPattern blocks: std::sto* and static_cast<T> moved from `member_patterns: [("","std::stoi"),...]` raw-substring fallback to `call_names: ["std::stoi","std::stol","std::stoul","std::stoll","std::stof","std::stod"]` and `call_names: ["static_cast<int>","static_cast<long>","static_cast<float>","static_cast<double>"]`.
additions_made.rationale: Gap 1 (M2-FIND-01). Raw-substring fallback in member_patterns_match (taint.rs:3094-3098) matched the descendant assignment node text including string-literal children (e.g. `std::string msg = "use std::stoi to convert";`). The per-descendant is_in_string filter at L3508/L3564 only excludes string nodes themselves, not their parent assignments. Moving entries to call_names restricts matching to call_node_kinds descendants only (call_expression for C++); string literals are not call_expression descendants, so the regression cannot recur. extract_call_name_c returns the function-field text verbatim — `std::stoi` for qualified_identifier and `static_cast<T>` for template_function — exactly matching the call_names entries.

discoveries.title: Latent string-literal-substring regression in raw-fallback path persists for the other 13 langs that still use raw substrings (Rust .parse::<>, Ruby CGI.escapeHTML, Elixir String.to_integer, etc.)
discoveries.summary: M2-FIND-01 was first surfaced for C++ (which had the most string-literally-collide-prone substring `std::stoi`). The same defect class exists for any AST_SANITIZERS entry with a non-empty pat_field and empty pat_rcv. C++ was fixed by Option C (move to call_names). Rust .parse::<i32> et al. cannot use call_names because turbofish is a generic_function shape that does not yield a clean function-field text matching `.parse::<i32>` exactly. M1 RED tests for those 13 langs were already FAILING at HEAD (regex still active matches inside string literals); they will GREEN at M4 when the regex is deleted, and the AST raw-fallback risk for those langs is gated by the per-descendant is_in_string filter — which catches the descendant being a string itself, but not the descendant being an assignment containing a string. A follow-up tightening to filter raw-fallback substring matches whose location overlaps a string-literal child is recommended for a future milestone but is OUT OF SCOPE for sanitizer-removal-v1 (would touch member_patterns_match, which is forbidden under standing rules).
discoveries.scope: 13 langs use raw-fallback in their AST sanitizer banks. Empirically, the M1 RED string-literal tests at M3 head FAIL for all 14 (regex still active); they will all PASS at M4 only if the AST raw-fallback shapes happen to NOT collide with their fixtures. Per M1 fixtures (which use bracketed-call shapes inside string literals like `"use int(x) to convert"`), descendant text includes the substring and the assignment is not in_string — so the regression risk is real and may surface at M4. M4 success criterion verifies. Documented here so M4 can address holistically (e.g. by extending detect_sanitizer_ast / build_sanitizer_ast_index to reject substring matches whose match span overlaps a string-literal child range, which is a caller-side change not member_patterns_match-side).
discoveries.owner_milestone: M4 (atomic deletion + full string-literal class coverage verification) or follow-up plan.

test_summary.ast_only: 20 passed / 0 failed (18 baseline + 2 new Zod tests). All sanitize_breaks_flow_<lang>_sanitizer_truncates_flow_ast_only GREEN.
test_summary.regular: 23 passed / 14 failed (1 PASS->improvement: cpp_std_stoi_in_string_literal_does_not_sanitize PASS [Gap 1 fixed]; 4 NEW: typescript_zod_parse + typescript_zod_safeParse on both regular and ast_only paths PASS; 14 string-literal RED gates still FAIL pre-M4 — expected, regex bank still active). Pre-M3 baseline at M2: 20 PASS / 15 FAIL. Net delta: +3 PASS (cpp string-literal regression closed + 2 Zod tests on regular path).
test_summary.regression_suites_green: All val* + rr_* + security/taint_tests preserved (sampled rr_baseline_per_language_test, val00*_taint, security/taint_tests via cargo test --tests aggregate).

compliance.no_regex_additions: OK (only existing regex Vecs; AST entries added only)
